content: src/projects/ module mirrors src/registry/'s file layout: errors.ts, config.ts (env vars), url.ts (parser), clone.ts (git shell-out), manage.ts (high-level operations), index.ts (re-exports), plus *.test.ts. Each high-level entry takes injectable spawn + filesystem stubs so tests don't shell out or touch disk.

title: Project clones are working trees, not caches
rationale: Unlike the canopy library clone (cached, hard-reset to origin/HEAD on every refresh), project clones are working trees that agents will actively run against. So src/projects/clone.ts deliberately does *not* support fast-forwarding an existing dir — it refuses with ProjectUnavailableError if the target path exists. Re-adding a project means explicit DELETE + re-clone, so the operator owns destructive resets.

title: deleteProject removes disk before row; addProject inserts row only after clone succeeds
rationale: Maintains the invariant 'a projects row exists ⇒ its localPath exists on disk'. addProject: validation/duplicate checks short-circuit before any disk work; clone failure rolls back any partial dir; row inserts only after clone returns success. deleteProject: rm-rf first, then repo.delete; if rm fails the row stays registered so the operator sees the failure and can retry, rather than ending up with a ghost row pointing at half a directory.

name: assertPathUnderRoot guard before rm-rf
description: deleteProject calls assertPathUnderRoot(row.localPath, config.root) before any rmrf. Uses path.resolve on both sides + startsWith(root + sep) check. Defense-in-depth: a tampered or stale db row whose localPath escaped /data/projects (e.g. '/etc/passwd') throws ProjectUnavailableError instead of letting warren rm-rf an arbitrary path. Test 'refuses to delete a project whose localPath escaped the configured root' enforces this.

content: GitHub URL parser (src/projects/url.ts) accepts only github.com hosts in three shapes: https://github.com/<owner>/<name>[.git], git@github.com:<owner>/<name>[.git], ssh://git@github.com/<owner>/<name>[.git]. Other hosts (gitlab, self-hosted) are rejected with ValidationError per SPEC §3.1's 'paste a GitHub URL' scope. Owner/name segments must match [A-Za-z0-9._-]+, may not be '.', '..', or start with '-' — keeps /data/projects/<owner>/<name> path safe.

Project management env vars: WARREN_PROJECTS_DIR (root for cloned project repos, default /data/projects) and WARREN_GIT_BINARY (default 'git', shared with the canopy registry — both modules read the same var so a single git binary override applies everywhere).

name: refresh-on-run
description: Project clones must be refreshed (git fetch + checkout + reset --hard origin/<ref>) before every spawnRun, otherwise /data/projects/<id>/ stays frozen at registration-time forever and agents work on stale code (warren-1bb6). The refresh runs BEFORE the warren run row is created so a fetch failure aborts the spawn cleanly without stranding a queued row. The default ref is the project's tracked default_branch; callers can override via POST /runs body.ref to point a run at a specific branch/tag/SHA. The same refreshProject() call backs the explicit POST /projects/:id/refresh endpoint.

title: Project clones are working trees that get re-fetched, not caches that get re-cloned
rationale: Operators register one project per repo and reuse it across many runs (per warren-1bb6 user feedback: 'I'm not going to create a new project for every PR/issue I work on, that defeats the whole purpose'). The lifecycle is: POST /projects = first-time clone, POST /projects/:id/refresh = git fetch + reset --hard origin/<ref>, DELETE = rm-rf + drop row. POST /runs implicitly refreshes before each dispatch. New columns last_fetched_at + last_head_sha let the UI surface 'is this clone current?' without re-shelling-out to git on every list. Hard-reset semantics (not pull/merge) match canopy's clone — warren never preserves agent-side commits across runs; that's burrow's job via the workspace branch.

content: refreshProjectClone runs git fetch --prune origin → git checkout --force <ref> → git reset --hard origin/<ref>, with a fallback to plain 'git reset --hard <ref>' when origin/<ref> doesn't resolve (so SHAs and tags work, not just remote branch names). HEAD sha is read via 'git rev-parse HEAD' after reset. Spawn + existsSync injection lets tests stub git without disk. ProjectUnavailableError is raised when the localPath has vanished (data-integrity issue, not a recoverable refresh).
